title: Ensure that encryption providers are appropriately configured

input_row.title: Ensure that encryption providers are appropriately configured
input_row.description: Where etcd encryption is used, appropriate providers should be configured.
input_row.rationale: Where etcd encryption is used, it is important to ensure that the appropriate set of encryption providers is used. Currently, the aescbc , kms and secretbox are likely to be appropriate options.
input_row.audit: OpenShift supports encryption of data at rest of etcd datastore, but it is up to the customer to configure. The asecbc cipher is used. No other ciphers are supported. Keys are stored on the filesystem of the master and automatically rotated. Run the following command to review the Encrypted status condition for the OpenShift API server to verify that its resources were successfully encrypted: # encrypt the etcd datastore oc get openshiftapiserver -o=jsonpath='{range .items[0].status.conditions[?(@.type=="Encrypted")]}{.reason}{"\n"}{.message}{"\n"}' 138 | P a g e The output shows EncryptionCompleted upon successful encryption. • EncryptionCompleted • All resources encrypted: routes.route.openshift.io, oauthaccesstokens.oauth.openshift.io, oauthauthorizetokens.oauth.openshift.io If the output shows EncryptionInProgress , this means that encryption is still in progress. Wait a few minutes and try again.
input_row.remediation: Follow the OpenShift documentation for Encrypting etcd data | Authentication | OpenShift Container Platform 4.5
input_row.impact: When you enable etcd encryption, the following OpenShift API server and Kubernetes API server resources are encrypted: • Secrets • ConfigMaps • Routes • OAuth access tokens • OAuth authorize tokens When you enable etcd encryption, encryption keys are created. These keys are rotated on a weekly basis. You must have these keys in order to restore from an etcd backup.
input_row.default_value: By default, no encryption provider is set.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Manual

**MANUAL STEPS:**
1. Open a terminal or command line that supports the OpenShift CLI (oc), and log into your OpenShift cluster.
2. Run the following command to check the encryption status of the OpenShift API server:
   ```
   oc get openshiftapiserver -o=jsonpath='{range .items[0].status.conditions[?(@.type=="Encrypted")]}{.reason}{"\n"}{.message}{"\n"}'
   ```
3. Verify the output of the command. You should see:
   - `EncryptionCompleted` if the encryption has been successfully enabled.
   - `All resources encrypted: routes.route.openshift.io, oauthaccesstokens.oauth.openshift.io, oauthauthorizetokens.oauth.openshift.io`
4. If the output shows `EncryptionInProgress`, then encoding is still being processed. Wait a few minutes and repeat step 2.
5. Review OpenShift documentation to ensure the appropriate encryption providers are configured as per the guidelines. The documentation can be accessed through the following URL: [OpenShift Encrypting etcd documentation](https://docs.openshift.com/container-platform/4.5/security/encrypting-etcd.html).
6. Confirm the configuration of encryption providers by checking the YAML or configuration files related to the etcd encryption settings to ensure they align with the best practices mentioned in the remediation section.

**EXPECTED SECURE CONFIGURATION:**
- The encryption status should be `EncryptionCompleted`.
- The proper encryption providers (aescbc, kms, and secretbox) should be configured accordingly.

**HOW TO CONFIRM COMPLIANCE:**
- Ensure that the output of your command matches the expected secure configuration to confirm that etcd encryption is enabled and is using appropriate providers. If the status is not correct, remediation steps should be initiated as per the OpenShift documentation.
